Description: User authentication and project foundation setup.
Acceptance Criteria: High level scope: provide secure authentication using Laravel Sanctum, foundational user model and permissions, and hooks for CI/deployment migrations.

Description: As a customer, I can register and log in to access my dashboard.
Acceptance Criteria:
Description:
- Backend: Implement POST /api/auth/register and POST /api/auth/login using Laravel Sanctum. Include validation (email, password strength), user creation, password hashing, and return an access token (personal access token or cookie+CSRF flow). Add migrations (users table + basic profile fields), unit tests, and API documentation (OpenAPI entries).
- Frontend: Create registration and login UI (Angular standalone components) with client-side validation, show clear error states, and store token/session securely. Implement 'remember me' optional and redirect to dashboard on success.
- DevOps: Add required environment variables (APP_URL, SANCTUM_STATEFUL_DOMAINS, SESSION_DOMAIN) to compose/dev setup and ensure migrations run at container start. Add CI job to run PHP unit tests and linting.
Suggested subtasks (create from these):
	- Backend: migration, controller, request validation, service + unit tests, OpenAPI spec entry
	- Frontend: login/register components, form validation, auth service, e2e test
	- DevOps: env config, container migration step, CI job for tests
Story Points:
Acceptance Criteria:
- POST /api/auth/register returns 201 and user id on valid payload; invalid payload returns 422 with error details.
- POST /api/auth/login returns 200 with access token (or sets auth cookie) and user payload for valid credentials; invalid creds return 401.
- Token-based auth allows accessing a protected endpoint /api/me which returns the authenticated user's profile.
- Frontend: user can register and log in via the UI, errors surfaced, redirected to /dashboard on success.
- Automated unit tests (backend) and at least one e2e test (frontend) covering login flow exist and pass in CI.
- DevOps: migrations executed at container startup in dev compose and CI runs tests for pull requests.

Description: Site creation and appliance catalog management.
Acceptance Criteria: High level: allow users to create a single site (MVP), store its metadata, and provide an appliance catalog for selection during estimation.

Description: As a customer, I can create one site with name/address/coords.
Acceptance Criteria:
Description:
- Backend: POST /api/sites accepts name, address, latitude, longitude, timezone, and optional notes. Validate address format and coords. Add site model/migration, relation to user, repository/service layer, and unit tests. Expose GET /api/sites and GET /api/sites/{id}.
- Frontend: Site creation form with map/coords entry (simple lat/long inputs acceptable for MVP). List sites on dashboard and allow selecting the active site for estimation.
- DevOps: Ensure DB migrations and seeders for demo data include a sample site. Document ENV or API base URL for frontend.
Epic,TUMI-E3,Estimation & Recommendation,Energy estimation and basic hardware recommendation."
Story Points:
- Estimation service computes totals
	- Backend: migration, model, controller, service, tests, OpenAPI addition
	- Frontend: site form component, site list, site-selection state in store
	- DevOps: seeder for demo site, update compose envs
Sprint:
Acceptance Criteria:
- POST /api/sites returns 201 and saved site including coords and linked user_id.
- GET /api/sites returns list including created site; GET /api/sites/{id} returns full details.
- Frontend: user can create a site and choose it for estimation; UI shows created site in list.
- Unit tests validate model relations; CI includes a smoke test to POST a sample site.

Description: As an admin, I seed and list appliances for users to select.
Acceptance Criteria:
Description:
- Backend: Create appliances table and seed script with fields: name, category, typical_wattage, usage_profile (hours/day), provider_id, SKU, and price. Provide GET /api/appliances (public read) and admin CRUD endpoints (protected).
- Frontend: Appliance catalog browse/list component and selection controls when building an estimation. Admin UI (minimal) to view and add appliances.
- DevOps: Migration and seeder included in container start and demo seed job.
- Recommendation suggests panel/inverter/battery

Issue Type: - DevOps: seed orchestration for demo environment
- Seeded appliances exist in DB and GET /api/appliances returns them with required fields.
- Admin CRUD endpoints require admin role and return 403 for non-admins.
- Frontend shows a list of appliances and allows selecting an appliance to add to a site estimation.
- Seeder can be run independently and is idempotent for demo purposes.

Acceptance Criteria:

- Persist estimation record
